records.ety: [F. ptition, L. petitio, fr. petere, petitum, to beg, ask, seek; perh. akin to E. feather, or find.]

defs: A prayer; a supplication; an imploration; an entreaty; especially, a request of a solemn or formal kind; a prayer to the Supreme Being, or to a person of superior power, rank, or authority; also, a single clause in such a prayer.
quotes: A house of prayer and petition for thy people.
This last petition heard of all her prayer.

defs: A formal written request addressed to an official person, or to an organized body, having power to grant it.

A request to government, in either of its branches, for the granting of a particular grace or right, or for the legislature to take a specific action; -- in distinction from a memorial, which calls certain facts to mind. The petition may be signed by one or any number of persons.
The written document containing a petition (senses 1 or 2).

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Petitioned (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Petitioning.]

defs: To make a prayer or request to; to ask from; to solicit; to entreat; especially, to make a formal written supplication, or application to, as to any branch of the government; as, to petition the court; to petition the governor.
quotes: You have . . . petitioned all the gods for my prosperity.

defs: To make a petition or solicitation.
